title: Chapter 46: The Captain's Choice
content: The rocket launcher changed everything.

Not because it had fired yet — Crane was too smart for that, too patient. He had it mounted on a technical at the back of his formation, a long metal finger pointing at the Citadel's main gate, and its presence alone was enough to make the defenders' stomachs clench.

One rocket. That's all it would take to breach the gate. And then the raiders would pour through, and the zombie moat wouldn't matter anymore, because the fight would be inside the walls where Sera couldn't use her army without hitting New Eden's own people.
Marcus knew it. Crane knew it. Everyone on both sides of the wall knew it.
But the raiders didn't know about the Multi-Partner Bond.

Through the link, Marcus could feel all four of his partners — their emotional states as clear as weather patterns on a map. Sera was the eye of a hurricane, calm and centered, her will stretched across the zombie moat like a bridge of ice. Junko was a lightning storm, crackling with battle focus, every muscle coiled for the next engagement. Elena was a steady warmth, methodical and grounding, sorting medical supplies like they were ammunition for a different kind of war. Vivienne was a wired circuit, processing radio signals and tactical data with the speed of someone who'd been doing this in a different context for years.
And somewhere beyond the wall, a fifth presence tugged at the edge of Marcus's awareness.
Captain Mira Santos.

She was on the roof of the bombed-out bank building, three hundred yards from the Citadel's eastern wall. She had a scoped rifle — a Remington 700, military grade, good for eight hundred meters. She'd had Marcus in her crosshairs three times this morning.
Three times, she hadn't pulled the trigger.

Through the Bond, Marcus couldn't read her — she wasn't a partner. But something about her emotional temperature registered on the periphery, like a draft from an open door. Not hostility. Not quite. Something closer to... hesitation.
"Vivienne," he said quietly. "Patch me into Crane's frequency. Open channel."
"Open channel? They'll hear you."
"That's the point."
Vivienne's fingers hovered over the shortwave controls. Then she nodded and flipped a switch. "You're live."
Marcus picked up the handset. "This is Marcus Chen, commander of New Eden. I'm addressing Captain Mira Santos."
Silence on the channel. Then — a burst of encrypted traffic from Crane's people. Orders. Panic.
Then a voice he hadn't heard before. Female. Controlled. With an undercurrent of something that might have been relief.
"This is Santos. Go ahead."
"You've had me in your scope three times this morning. You haven't fired. I'm guessing that's not because you're a bad shot."
A pause. "No."
"Then why?"
Another pause. Longer this time. When she spoke again, her voice was barely above a whisper, as if she was afraid someone on her own side might hear.

"Because I've been watching your people. The zombie queen who fights to protect. The nurse who treats enemy wounded. The aristocrat who swallowed her pride for strangers. The martial artist who disarms instead of kills." A breath. "And the man who walks out of his fortress to face an army alone because he'd rather die than let someone else take the risk."
Through the Bond, Marcus felt the reaction from his partners — Sera's fierce pride, Elena's blush, Junko's surprised grunt, Vivienne's quiet satisfaction.

"Crane keeps women in a stable," Mira continued, her voice steadying. "I've been telling myself it's different. That they chose it. That I'm not complicit. But I've seen the ones who try to leave, and I've seen what happens to them." Her voice cracked, just barely. "I can't do this anymore. Not after watching what you've built."
"Mira." Marcus kept his voice calm. "You don't have to join us. You just have to stop helping him."
Silence. The wind across the rooftop. The distant rumble of Crane's vehicles.
Then: "Crane to all units. Santos is compromised. Take her into custody."
On the open channel, the order landed like a grenade. Marcus heard shouting — Crane's lieutenants barking orders, boots on metal staircases, the clatter of weapons being readied.
"Captain." Crane's voice was deceptively soft. "You had one job. One. And you couldn't even—"
"I quit, Victor." Mira's voice was clear and final. "I quit three weeks ago. I just didn't have anywhere to go until now."
Twelve seconds of silence.
Then, one by one, they started walking.

The first was a man — mid-thirties, AK-47 held over his head by the barrel. He walked out of the raider formation, across the no-man's-land, through the zombie moat (which parted for him like it had parted for Sera, because Marcus had told her through the Bond to let them through), and up to the Citadel gate.
"I'm done," he said. "Where do you want me?"
The second was a woman — young, maybe twenty-two, carrying a medical kit. She looked at Elena through the gate and said, "I was pre-med. Before."
The third. The fourth. The fifth.

Twelve of them. Twelve raiders who'd been waiting for someone to go first, and Mira Santos had given them permission. They walked through the zombie moat with their hands up and their weapons down, and New Eden's people took them in.

Mira came last. She walked through the dead like a woman walking through a dream, her rifle slung across her back, her face set in the hard mask of someone who'd made an irreversible choice and was still processing it.
Marcus was waiting at the gate.
"Your rifle," he said.
She unslung it and handed it over, butt-first. He took it.
"Welcome to New Eden, Captain."
She almost smiled. "It's just Mira. I don't work for anyone anymore."
"That's the point," he said, echoing her own words back at her.

Through the Bond, he felt Sera's grudging respect, Elena's immediate acceptance, Junko's evaluation (she'd seen Mira fight from a distance and was impressed), and Vivienne's practical assessment (former military, useful skills).

On the other side of the wall, Crane's formation was in chaos. Thirty of his best — gone. Not killed. Not captured. *Voluntarily departed*. For a man who ruled through fear, there was no greater insult.
"They're regrouping," Vivienne reported. "Crane is... he's not retreating. He's pulling his elite forward. He's going to lead the breach himself."
Marcus looked at the rocket launcher through the binoculars. Then at Crane, standing on the technical's bed, his shaved head catching the morning sun, his expression unreadable at this distance.
The siege wasn't stalling anymore. It was escalating.
And the rocket launcher was still pointed at the gate.

**[RAIDER FORCE: 40% EFFECTIVE — 12 DEFECTED]**
**[NEW EDEN: +12 RECRUITS (including Captain Mira Santos)]**
**[MIRA SANTOS: Potential Ally registered]**
**[WARNING: Crane preparing direct assault with elite forces]**
